session.user:
⏺ User answered Claude's questions: ⎿ · 📋 Plan: Deterministic planning run ID from structured metadata Current context: (wt:erk-slot-42) (br:plnd/show-planning-run-id-03-10-1209) (pr:#9175)
What would you like to do with this plan? → Create new branch and planned PR
⏺ Skill(/erk:plan-save) ⎿ Successfully loaded skill
⏺ Step 1: No special flags passed.
Step 2: Branch slug from "Deterministic planning run ID from structured metadata": deterministic-run-id-field
Step 3: Summary: "Adds a first-class created_from_workflow_run_id field to the plan header schema so the TUI dashboard can display the planning run ID deterministically from structured metadata instead of parsing it from the workflow run URL."
Step 4: Running the save command.
⏺ Bash(erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a" --branch-slug="deterministic-run-id-field" --summary="Adds a first-class created_from_workflow_run_id field to the plan header schema so the TUI dashboard can display the planning run ID deterministically from structured metadata instead of parsing it from the workflow run URL.") ⎿ { "success": true, "pr_number": 9206, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9206", "title": "[erk-pr] Plan: Deterministic planning run ID from structured metadata", "branch_name": "plnd/deterministic-run-id-field-03-11-1146", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-42/.erk/scratch/sessions/748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a/plan_snapshots/000001-5c82aa7b" } ⎿ (timeout 1m)
⏺ Plan saved. No objective linked, skipping verification.
Step 5: Pushing planning session.
⏺ Bash(erk exec capture-session-info 2>/dev/null) ⎿ SESSION_ID=748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a SESSION_FILE=/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn--erk-repos-erk-worktrees-erk-slot-42/748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a.jsonl
⏺ Bash(erk exec push-session --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn--erk-repos-erk-worktrees-erk-slot-42/748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a.jsonl" --session-id "748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a" --stage planning --source local --pr-number 9206 2>/dev/null || true) ⎿ { "uploaded": true, "pr_number": 9206, "session_branch": "planned-pr-context/9206", "session_id": "748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a", "stage": "planning", "files": [ "planning-748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a-748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a-part1.xml", "planning-748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a-748fc374-c6a8-4910-bf7f-b658479ad53a-part2.xml" ] } ⎿ (timeout 30s)
⏺ Plan "Deterministic planning run ID from structured metadata" saved as draft PR #9206 URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9206
⏺ Next steps for PR #9206:
Implement PR #9206: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9206 && erk implement (dangerously): erk br co --for-plan 9206 && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9206 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9206 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout PR #9206: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 9206 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 9206
Dispatch PR #9206: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 9206 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
✻ Sautéed for 4m 9s
the language in the user-facing text here should be "planned pr" rather than "plan"

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Exit Plan Mode Hook. 3→ 4→Prompts user before exiting plan mode when a plan exists. This hook intercepts 5→the ExitPlanMode tool via PreToolUse lifecycle to ask whether to save to GitHub 6→or implement immediately. 7→ 8→Exit codes: 9→ 0: Success (allow exit - no plan, implement-now marker present, or no session) 10→ 2: Block (plan exists, no implement-now marker - prompt user) 11→ 12→This command is invoked via: 13→ erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook 14→ 15→Marker File State Machine 16→========================= 17→ 18→This hook uses marker files in .erk/scratch/sessions/<session-id>/ for state management. 19→Marker files are self-describing: their names indicate their purpose and their contents 20→explain their effect. 21→ 22→Marker Files: 23→ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now.marker 24→ Created by: Agent (when user chooses "Implement without saving") 25→ Effect: Next ExitPlanMode call is ALLOWED (exit plan mode, proceed to implementation) 26→ Lifecycle: Deleted after being read by next hook invocation 27→ 28→ exit-plan-mode-hook.plan-saved.marker 29→ Created by: /erk:plan-save command (both new-branch and current-branch paths) 30→ Content: First line is the plan PR number

Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan). 20→ 21→## Plan Storage 22→ 23→The plan is saved using the configured backend: 24→ 25→- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. .claude/skills/erk-planning/references/workflow.md:41:- Plan will be saved to `~/.claude/plans/` .claude/skills/session-inspector/references/format.md:187:- Added when Plan Mode is exited (plan approved and saved) .claude/skills/command-creator/references/examples.md:413:4. **Plan is saved to disk** as a markdown file .claude/commands/erk/plan-save.md:169:Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)` .claude/commands/erk/plan-save.md:176:Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> docs/TAO.md:25:Engineers who want to work in a plan-oriented way face significant friction. Plans are saved as markdown files and must be managed manually, or exist only ephemerally in agent context. There is no system of record. Plans cannot be queried, tracked, or closed. They are not attached to any automation or workflow. docs/tutorials/first-plan.md:85:Plan saved as issue #1 docs/learned/reference/gastown-analysis.md:693:| Propulsion | (weak: plan mode) | Plan mode implements partial propulsion for saved plans | docs/learned/cli/plan-implement.md:181:1. Plan content has been saved to GitHub (permanent storage) docs/learned/objectives/roadmap-mutation-patterns.md:36:| Plan saved to GitHub | Surgical | Only the status of one step changes to in_progress | docs/learned/planning/branch-name-inference.md:24:1. **Plan saved** → GitHub issue created (no branch exists yet) docs/learned/sessions/layout.md:91:When Plan Mode is exited (user approves plan), the assistant entry includes a `slug` field identifying the saved plan: docs/learned/sessions/layout.md:113:- **Timing**: Added when Plan Mode is exited (plan approved and saved) docs/learned/hooks/hooks.md:352:# Example: Plan already saved to GitHub docs/learned/hooks/hooks.md:355: click.echo("✅ Plan saved to GitHub. Session complete.") docs/learned/hooks/pretooluse-implementation.md:64:Exit code 2 is reserved for hooks where the tool's default behavior is actively unwanted (e.g., the ExitPlanMode hook blocks plan approval when a plan was already saved to GitHub). docs/learned/planning/scratch-storage.md:94:Plans created in Claude Code are saved to scratch storage for session-scoped lookup: docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md:22:When Claude retries a blocked ExitPlanMode call, the same plan could be saved multiple times, creating duplicate GitHub issues. This can happen when: docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md:73:The `plan-saved` marker persists because it represents a state ("plan has been saved") not an action. If the session continues after saving, subsequent ExitPlanMode calls should still know the plan was saved. docs/learned/planning/session-deduplication.md:80:2. Subsequent ExitPlanMode calls need to know plan is already saved

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/unified-launching-newt.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Use "planned PR" terminology in user-facing output Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
# Use "planned PR" terminology in user-facing output
## Context
The user-facing output after saving a plan says `Plan "..." saved as draft PR #9206` and uses bare `Implement PR #`, `Checkout PR #`, `Dispatch PR #` headers. The correct erk domain term for the GitHub PR entity is "planned PR", not "plan".
## Changes
### 1. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/output/next_steps.py` - `Implement PR #` → `Implement planned PR #` - `Checkout PR #` → `Checkout planned PR #` - `Dispatch PR #` → `Dispatch planned PR #` - `**Checkout PR branch:**` → `**Checkout planned PR branch:**`
### 2. `.claude/commands/erk/plan-save.md` - `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)` → `Planned PR #<pr_number> already saved (duplicate skipped)` - `Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number>` → `Planned PR "<title>" saved as #<pr_number>`
### 3. Test updates (5 assertions across 3 files)
## Verification ```bash uv run pytest tests/unit/shared/test_next_steps.py tests/commands/pr/test_create.py tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_exit_plan_mode_hook.py -x ```

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'What would you like to do with this plan?', 'header': 'br:master', 'options...[truncated 283 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `pr_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <pr_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<pr_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<pr_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → PR #<pr_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as PR #<pr_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<pr_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `pr_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <pr_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<pr_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Step 1: No special flags passed.
Step 2: Branch slug from "Use 'planned PR' terminology in user-facing output": `planned-pr-terminology`
Step 3: Summary: "Updates user-facing output strings to use the domain term planned PR instead of bare plan or PR when referring to the GitHub PR entity created from a plan. Changes section headers in next-steps output and display templates in the plan-save skill."
Step 4: Running the save command.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88" --bra...[truncated 182 chars]...hanges section headers in next-steps output and display templates in the plan-save skill."

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "pr_number": 9209, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9209", "title": "[erk-pr] Use \"planned PR\" terminology in user-facing output", "branch_name": "plnd/planned-pr-terminology-03-11-1158", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/54671ef7-c44d-4143-a4d6-fc964f75cc88/plan_snapshots/000001-f6eb3356"}
session.assistant:
Plan saved. No objective linked, skipping verification.
Step 5: Pushing planning session.
